REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE (TRANSACTION ID # 61284902) FILED BY RESPONDENT TOBY DOUGLAS, AS DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER HOAG MEMORIAL HOSPITAL PRESBYTERIAN'S PETITION FOR ADMINISTRATIVE WRIT OF MANDAMUS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1094.5. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES THE TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER HOAG MEMORIAL HOSPITAL PRESBYTERIAN'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE PURSUANT TO CCP 1094.5 FOR REVIEW OF FINAL DECISION OF DIRECTOR OF HEALTH CARE AND SERVICES DENYING PAYING FOR MEDICAL INPATIENT SERVICES AGAINST TOBY DOUGLAS, AS DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES IS DENIED ONLY AS TO "STATEMENT OF DISPUTED ISSUES-2" ("SODI-2"). AS TO "STATEMENT OF DISPUTED ISSUES-1" ("SODI-1") THE COURT VACATES THE TENTATIVE RULING AND REVERSES THE DISMISSAL BY THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE (ALJ). THE COURT INSTRUCTS THE ALJ TO HOLD SODI-1 IN ABEYANCE PENDING CONCLUSION OF LITIGATION IN COURT OF APPEAL. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET, CONFER AND AGREE ON A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Petition For Administrative Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1094.5
Off Calendar Nov-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, [ ] of the law offices of [ ] ( ) appears as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

Attorney, [ ] of the law offices of [ ] ( ) appears as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court vacates the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER HOAG MEMORIAL HOSPITAL PRESBYTERIAN'S Petition For Administrative Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1094.5.

Petitioner Hoag Memorial Hospital Presbyterian's petition for writ of mandate pursuant to CCP 1094.5 for review of final decision of Director of Health Care and Services denying paying for medical inpatient services against Toby Douglas, as Director of the California Department of Health Care Services is DENIED only as to "Statement of Disputed Issues-2" ("SODI-2"). As to "Statement of Disputed Issues-1" ("SODI-1") the Court vacates the tentative ruling and reverses the dismissal by the administrative law judge (ALJ). The Court instructs the ALJ to hold SODI-1 in abeyance pending conclusion of litigation in Court of Appeal. The parties shall meet, confer and agree on a [proposed] written order. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Hearing On Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Petition For Administrative Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1094.5
Off Calendar Sep-18-2017 Continued To Nov-07-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
